records.ety: [F. rsider, L. residere; pref. re- re- + sedere to sit. See Sit. ]
records.morph: [imp. & p. p. Resided; p. pr. & vb. n. Residing.]

defs: To dwell permanently or for a considerable time; to have a settled abode for a time; to abide continuosly; to have one's domicile of home; to remain for a long time.
quotes: At the moated grange, resides this dejected Mariana.
In no fixed place the happy souls reside.

defs: To have a seat or fixed position; to inhere; to lie or be as in attribute or element.
quotes: In such like acts, the duty and virtue of contentedness doth especially reside.

defs: To sink; to settle, as sediment.
